text.title: AI-Assisted Multi-Squad Quarter Retro Synthesis Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Chat / ticket / incident text MUST be redacted (PII, client names, secrets) BEFORE any LLM call. Redaction runs locally.
rule.rationale: Quarterly corpora contain customer names and credentials; pre-LLM redaction is the only safe surface.

rule.statement: DORA deltas (lead time / change-failure / deploy frequency / MTTR) come from the snapshot CSV, NEVER from LLM inference.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate numbers; metrics must be deterministic.

rule.statement: Every theme cites ≥ 2 verbatim quotes from source documents with locator.
rule.rationale: Same anti-hallucination discipline as the annual synthesis.

rule.statement: Each incident referenced in the report links back to its postmortem doc with locator.
rule.rationale: Without the link, the agent's incident narrative becomes its own truth.

rule.statement: Each action item names role + deadline ≤ end of next quarter + success criteria.
If < 3 squads OR DORA not instrumented OR HR-confidential incidents present, the agent MUST stop.
rule.rationale: Skip preserves the synthesis's integrity rather than fabricating.
